message:
feat(core/live-vtab): implement streaming live-vtab INSERT SELECT fast path

Previous commit introduced the `try_execute_streaming_live_vtab_insert_select`
stub in the dispatch layer but routed through the existing
`collect_live_vtab_insert_rows` until the helper could actually stream.
This commit lands the real streaming implementation and refactors the
row-build code so both the streaming and materialized paths share a
single source of truth for layout resolution and row construction.

## Refactor: split collect into (resolve_layout, build_row, collect_rows)

- New `resolve_live_vtab_insert_layout(insert) -> LiveVtabInsertLayout`
  does the one-time schema walk: reads the default SQL expressions for
  every column and builds the source→target column index map. Returns
  the struct introduced in the prior commit (`default_sqls`,
  `source_targets`). Runs once per statement instead of once per row.

- New `build_live_vtab_insert_row(&layout, source_values, context)`
  takes a resolved layout plus one row of source values and produces a
  `LiveVtabInsertRow { explicit_rowid, values }`. Previously, this was
  a closure (`build_row`) captured over `default_sqls`/`source_targets`
  inside `collect_live_vtab_insert_rows`; lift it to a method so the
  streaming path can call it without having to clone the layout into a
  closure. Same column-count-mismatch error text, same fill-once
  semantics, same explicit-rowid handling.

- `collect_live_vtab_insert_rows` is now a thin orchestrator that
  calls `resolve_live_vtab_insert_layout` once, then dispatches the
  three source variants (Values, Select, DefaultValues) through
  `build_live_vtab_insert_row`. No user-visible behavior change for
  the materialized path.

## try_execute_streaming_live_vtab_insert_select: real implementation

- Bail out to `Ok(None)` if:
    * source is not `InsertSource::Select(_)`, or
    * a WITH clause is present (CTE sources require materialization).
- Resolve the target table's root page from the schema and open a
  new pager btree write cursor under `with_pager_write_txn`.
- Hold the live vtab instance across the streaming loop so the
  `update(cx, args)` calls cluster on one transaction. Call
  `begin_live_vtab_transaction_if_needed` once up front.
- Special-case r-tree virtual tables via
  `instance.as_any().is::<RtreeVirtualTable>()` — r-tree inserts need
  the explicit rowid to be copied from the first column when the
  caller did not provide an explicit rowid, so the streaming path
  does the same first-column patch the materialized path already did.
- Call `try_stream_simple_join_select_rows` to iterate the SELECT's
  rows in streaming fashion. Per row:
    1. Build a LiveVtabInsertRow via `build_live_vtab_insert_row`.
    2. Construct the `args` vector for `instance.update`: `[NULL,
       new_rowid, ...values]`.
    3. Drive `instance.update(cx, &args)` which performs the actual
       live-vtab insert and returns the assigned rowid.
    4. If `patch_first_column_with_rowid` applied and the first
       column came back NULL, patch it to the returned rowid.
    5. Shadow the live vtab's row into the backing btree: delete
       any existing row at that rowid (idempotency for re-inserts
       from the same SELECT), then `table_insert` the serialized
       record.
    6. Record the new rowid as the statement's last_insert_rowid.
- If `try_stream_simple_join_select_rows` returned false (the join
  shape wasn't stream-compatible), return `Ok(None)` so the caller
  falls back to the materialized path without recording any changes.

## Minor cleanups in the same file

- Replace `let InsertSource::Select(_) = &insert.source` inside a
  let-chain condition with `matches!(insert.source,
  InsertSource::Select(_))`. The let-chain form was taking an
  unnecessary reference for a discriminant check; `matches!` reads
  cleaner here since none of the inner data is used.
- One rustfmt reflow where `try_stream_simple_join_select_rows` was
  split across lines with inconsistent indentation.
